sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: The upcoming clash between Everton and Ipswich Town on May 3, 2025, at Goodison Park in Liverpool promises to be an intriguing encounter in Round 35 of the Premier League. Everton, currently sitting at 15th in the league with 38 points, will host Ipswich Town, who are struggling in 18th place with just 21 points. With the season nearing its end, both teams are desperate for points, but for very different reasons—Everton to secure mid-table safety and Ipswich to climb out of the relegation zone. This match at the iconic Goodison Park, refereed by Lewis Smith, who averages 3.7 yellow cards per game, could see a tense battle unfold.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Looking at their historical matchups, Everton hold a slight edge over Ipswich Town in recent times. In their last 5 encounters, Everton have secured 3 wins, while Ipswich managed 1 victory, with 1 game ending in a draw. Their most recent meeting on October 19, 2024, saw Everton triumph 2-0 away at Ipswich, with Michael Keane earning the Player of the Match title with a Sofascore rating of 7.8. This result could give Everton a psychological boost heading into this game.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Everton’s recent form at home shows a trend of low-scoring games, with their last 8 home matches featuring under 2.5 goals. They’ve also been the first to concede in 5 of their last 6 matches, which could be a concern against an Ipswich side desperate to score. On the other hand, Ipswich Town are on a worrying streak of 4 games without a win and have failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 16 matches. Additionally, Ipswich’s last 6 games have seen over 2.5 goals, contrasting sharply with Everton’s defensive solidity at home, which might lead to an interesting tactical battle.

sections.subtitle: Everton Key Players and Stats
sections.text: Everton’s squad boasts some standout performers who could make a difference in this match. Iliman Ndiaye, a midfielder with a league rank of 4 for successful dribbles per game at 2.1, has scored 7 goals this season against an expected goals tally of 5.18. Jarrad Branthwaite, ranked 5th in the league for ground duels won at 72.2%, will be key in defense. Michael Keane, ranked 1st for the percentage of long balls in total passes at 12.2%, offers a direct approach from the back. However, the absence of defender James Tarkowski, who has a league rank of 5 for aerial duels won at 74.6%, could weaken their backline.

sections.subtitle: Ipswich Town Key Players and Stats
sections.text: Ipswich Town will rely heavily on forward Liam Delap, who has outperformed his expected goals of 9.10 by scoring 12 goals this season, making him a significant threat upfront. Defender Cameron Burgess, with a league rank of 4 for ground duels won at 75%, could be crucial in containing Everton’s attackers. However, Ipswich will miss several key players like Sammie Szmodics, who has scored 4 goals, and Omari Hutchinson with 3 goals, which might hamper their attacking options. Jack Clarke, with 4 assists, will need to step up in their absence.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Team Statistics
sections.text: Everton’s season stats highlight their defensive strengths, with a league rank of 4 for least goals conceded at 41 and ranking 2nd for clearances per game at 28.29. They also excel in interceptions per game at 8.79, ranking 3rd in the league, which could disrupt Ipswich’s rhythm. However, their attacking output is a concern, with only 34 goals scored, ranking 17th, and a mere 30.3% big chance conversion rate, the 18th worst in the league. Ipswich, on the other hand, have shown a knack for late goals, with 21.6% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes, which could test Everton’s concentration late in the game.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Trends
sections.text: Discipline could play a big role in this match, with referee Lewis Smith averaging 3.7 yellow cards per game, though both teams have a head-to-head trend of under 4.5 cards in their last 9 meetings. Everton commit fouls frequently, ranking 5th with 394 fouls this season, and have received 72 yellow cards, ranking 9th for least cards. Set-piece opportunities might be limited, with Everton averaging just 4.12 corners per game (14th in the league), while Ipswich’s games often see high corner counts, with over 10.5 corners in their last 5 matches.

sections.subtitle: Historical Standout Player
sections.text: In matches against Ipswich Town over the last 5 encounters, Everton’s Ben Godfrey has been a consistent performer, starting in 4 of those games with an average Sofascore rating of 7.18. While he hasn’t contributed with goals or assists in these fixtures, his reliability in defense or midfield could be a quiet but important factor if he features in this matchup. His experience against Ipswich could provide Everton with a tactical edge.
